toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run the tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected using environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables crash dumps
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump

Alternatively, since this project uses the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is true), there might be a `--crashdump` option available.


The Microsoft Testing Platform supports `--crashdump` as a command-line option, but the more standard approach with `dotnet test` is using the `--blame-crash` flag, which automatically captures a crash dump if the test host process crashes.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-13ec12e8eeeb4eef92205fdb23efa304 && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command used:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag tells the test runner to collect a crash dump (via `dotnet-dump`) if the test host process crashes unexpectedly. Since no crash occurred here, no dump was generated. If a test were to crash the host, the dump would be saved under the `TestResults/` directory.
